2605.30107.title: Dial HEALTHDIAL for Advice: A Multilingual and Multi-Parallel Spoken Dialogue Dataset for Knowledge-Grounded Information Seeking
2605.30107.paper_summary: Creating spoken dialogue datasets is methodologically challenging, and these challenges are amplified when the goal is to build multilingual, multi-parallel datasets at scale. This work introduces HEALTHDIAL, a large-scale, multilingual, and multi-parallel dataset for developing and evaluating retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)-based spoken dialogue systems. The dataset comprises 6,000 information-seeking dialogues (1,500 per language) grounded in trusted content from the World Health Organization (WHO) and 163 hours of user speech recorded from native speakers of diverse dialects across four official WHO languages: Arabic, Chinese, English, and Spanish. Each speaker is annotated with demographic (e.g., gender, age) and sociolinguistic (e.g., primary language, region of origin) variables. We report benchmark results across key dialogue tasks, which reveal consistent performance disparities across languages, even among high-resource ones. To support future research, we release the dataset, a prototype system, and a toolkit for data collection and system evaluation.
2605.30107.paper_summary_zh: 建立口語對話數據集在方法上具有挑戰性，當目標是大規模構建多語言、多平行數據集時，這些挑戰會加劇。這項工作介紹了HEALTHDIAL，一個大規模的多語言和多平行數據集，用於開發和評估基於檢索增強生成（RAG）的口語對話系統。該數據集包含6,000個尋求信息的對話（每種語言1,500個），基於來自世界衛生組織（WHO）的可信內容，以及從四種官方WHO語言的多種方言的母語者錄製的163小時用戶語音：阿拉伯語、中文、英語和西班牙語。每位說話者都被標註了人口統計（例如，性別、年齡）和社會語言學（例如，主要語言、來源地區）變量。我們報告了關鍵對話任務的基準結果，顯示出不同語言之間的一致性能差異，即使在高資源語言之間也是如此。為了支持未來的研究，我們發布了數據集、一個原型系統以及一個數據收集和系統評估的工具包。
2605.30107.author: Songbo Hu et.al.
2605.30107.authors: Songbo Hu, Yinhong Liu, Ej Zhou, Evgeniia Razumovskaia, Xiaobin Wang, Alexander Fraser, Ivan Vulić, Anna Korhonen
